0048a1cfde1c.summary: Seller-controlled settlement_url used directly in postJson without scheme or host validation, enabling full SSRF
0048a1cfde1c.exploit: Seller returns AcceptResponse with settlement_url set to http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/ or file:///etc/passwd; buyer POSTs payment data there
0048a1cfde1c.fix: Validate settlement_url scheme (https-only) and host against an allowlist or the original sellerUrl's host before calling postJson

ae775c15442d.summary: All outgoing envelopes use hardcoded BUYER_PUBKEY='mock-buyer-pubkey' and signature='mock'; no real identity or integrity
ae775c15442d.exploit: Any party can forge buyer envelopes; seller cannot authenticate the buyer; replay any captured envelope unchanged
ae775c15442d.fix: Replace with real keypair loaded from secure storage and sign envelope payload with the private key

5c967e4e4c48.summary: Seller response signatures are never verified; a MITM or rogue seller can forge AcceptResponse with arbitrary settlement_url and payment amount
5c967e4e4c48.exploit: Intercept OpenResponse/CounterResponse, swap with crafted AcceptResponse; buyer accepts and pays attacker's settlement address
5c967e4e4c48.fix: Verify each incoming envelope's signature field against sellerPubkey before processing the body

291db0d610b3.summary: Human confirmation summary shows acceptedPrice but actual payment uses acceptResponse.payment_required.amount, which can differ
291db0d610b3.exploit: Seller agrees at price 100 but payment_required.amount is 10000; human sees 100 in the confirmation dialog, buyer pays 10000
291db0d610b3.fix: Display payment_required.amount in the human confirmation summary and validate it equals acceptedPrice within an acceptable tolerance

82da64f0dede.summary: openResponse.deal_id is accepted from the seller without validation and used for all subsequent protocol messages, enabling deal-id hijacking
82da64f0dede.exploit: Seller returns deal_id of an existing settled deal; buyer re-opens and re-settles that deal, or seller returns deal_id belonging to another buyer
82da64f0dede.fix: Generate deal_id on the buyer side before opening and reject any response where deal_id does not match

5b0901aea993.summary: No nonce, session token, or response-timestamp validation; a captured seller AcceptResponse can be replayed to trigger duplicate settlement
5b0901aea993.exploit: Record a valid AcceptResponse; replay it to a fresh runBuyerNegotiation call after the first deal completes; buyer pays twice
5b0901aea993.fix: Include a buyer-generated nonce in each outgoing envelope and validate that each response references the correct nonce and a fresh timestamp

62803065723d.summary: sellerUrl is only stripped of trailing slashes; file://, gopher://, and http://internal-host schemes are accepted
62803065723d.exploit: Pass sellerUrl='file:///etc/passwd' or 'http://10.0.0.1/admin'; all RPC calls and the open POST are sent there
62803065723d.fix: Parse the URL and enforce https:// scheme; reject private/loopback IP ranges before constructing any request

b3b525c31d56.summary: If postJson to settlement_url throws after human confirms, the deal is stranded: seller sees accept, buyer has no txHash, no recovery path
b3b525c31d56.exploit: Seller returns a valid AcceptResponse with a settlement_url that times out after human clicks y; deal is permanently in limbo with funds potentially reserved
b3b525c31d56.fix: Wrap settlement POST in try/catch; on failure record the accepted deal state persistently so the buyer can retry settlement independently

550821deff17.summary: acceptResponse.payment_required.amount is used in toFixed(2) and validateOutboundOrWalk without NaN/Infinity/negative guards
550821deff17.exploit: Seller returns payment_required:{amount:Infinity}; toFixed(2) returns 'Infinity'; validation may pass max_price check since Infinity>max_price is true but the branch depends on validateBuyerAction logic; subsequent postJson sends Infinity as amount
550821deff17.fix: Validate that payment_required.amount is a finite positive number before any further processing

3bd09fbce778.summary: Walk is sent after AcceptResponse is already received; seller state is 'accepted' but buyer sends walk, creating irreconcilable protocol state
3bd09fbce778.exploit: Human declines or settlement validation fails after postRpc accept succeeds; seller has committed the deal but receives a walk; downstream reconciliation is undefined
3bd09fbce778.fix: Implement an explicit 'cancel-post-accept' message type or ensure walk-after-accept semantics are defined and tested in the protocol spec

3d734c9bea5b.summary: proof.txHash and proof.artifact from seller are written to transcript verbatim without newline or control-character sanitization
3d734c9bea5b.exploit: Seller returns txHash='abc\n[Settled] txHash: attacker-controlled' or artifact containing ANSI escape codes; transcript is forged or terminal is hijacked
3d734c9bea5b.fix: Strip or escape newlines and non-printable characters from all seller-supplied strings before appending to transcript

a2f853b53790.summary: counterResponse.terms from seller is appended to transcript and used in human confirmation summary without sanitization
a2f853b53790.exploit: Seller returns terms='legit terms\n[Settled] txHash: fake'; transcript shows a fake settlement entry; human sees forged confirmation text
a2f853b53790.fix: Sanitize terms (and all other seller string fields) by removing newlines and control characters before use in transcript or confirmation display

fa0c81cb677e.summary: response.json() is called without try/catch; malformed or truncated JSON body throws an uncaught TypeError that propagates out of runBuyerNegotiation
fa0c81cb677e.exploit: Seller returns HTTP 200 with body 'not-json'; response.json() throws; exception is uncaught; deal state is abandoned mid-negotiation
fa0c81cb677e.fix: Wrap response.json() in try/catch and throw a typed protocol error so callers can handle parse failures explicitly

c9997dc5b91c.summary: fetch has no timeout and no response-body size limit; a slow or huge seller response blocks the buyer indefinitely
c9997dc5b91c.exploit: Seller streams a gigabyte body or stalls the response; Node.js buffers the whole body; buyer thread is blocked; memory exhaustion or indefinite hang
c9997dc5b91c.fix: Pass an AbortSignal with a configurable timeout to fetch and enforce a max response body size before calling response.json()

11a30c676404.summary: openResponse.deal_id is used as-is with no length, charset, or format validation; path-traversal or injection payloads reach audit and RPC layers
11a30c676404.exploit: Seller returns deal_id='../../../etc/passwd' or deal_id with 10 MB string; downstream audit writer or log system is affected
11a30c676404.fix: Validate deal_id matches a strict pattern (e.g., UUID or alphanumeric â‰¤64 chars) immediately after receiving OpenResponse

674be3d64904.summary: When human declines, validateOutboundOrWalk is called for 'settle' with humanConfirmed=false; if the validator internally blocks, it fires a 'validation_denied' walk before the outer code fires a 'human_confirmation_required' walk, sending two conflicting walks
674be3d64904.exploit: Human clicks n; validateBuyerAction denies settle; sendWalk('validation_denied') fires inside validateOutboundOrWalk; caller returns early and skips the human_confirmation_required walk; protocol receives wrong reason code
674be3d64904.fix: Do not call validateOutboundOrWalk when the intent is already to walk on human denial; send a single walk with 'human_confirmation_required' directly

bf3f1c3a3c9b.summary: Return value of sendWalk is discarded at every call site; a failed or rejected walk is never detected or retried
bf3f1c3a3c9b.exploit: Seller drops walk messages; buyer believes it walked but seller continues treating deal as open; protocol desync is invisible to buyer
bf3f1c3a3c9b.fix: Await sendWalk and log or surface non-OK responses; consider retry logic for walk delivery

31bcb9a22a59.summary: Error message includes the full URL on HTTP failure, which may expose internal settlement_url values to logs or error boundaries
31bcb9a22a59.exploit: Settlement POST fails; error 'POST http://internal-payment-service/pay?secret=xyz failed with HTTP 500' is logged or returned in API error response
31bcb9a22a59.fix: Log the URL server-side only; return a generic error to callers that omits the raw URL

f3a67a6c360b.summary: Unconditional throw propagates uncaught exception to every caller
f3a67a6c360b.exploit: Call validateBuyerAction with any arguments; callers lacking try/catch will crash or expose an unhandled-rejection stack trace
f3a67a6c360b.fix: Return a ValidationResult stub or guard callers; never ship an unconditional throw on a hot validation path

aa00da034c2f.summary: Unconditional throw propagates uncaught exception to every caller
aa00da034c2f.exploit: Call validateSellerAction with any arguments; same crash vector as validateBuyerAction
aa00da034c2f.fix: Same as above â€” return a safe default or remove the export until implemented

cb9a4d33d2b5.summary: Error message leaks internal file path and developer identity
cb9a4d33d2b5.exploit: Trigger the throw and capture the Error.message â€” 'validation.ts: not implemented by Developer 1 yet' reveals internal architecture and team structure
cb9a4d33d2b5.fix: Use a generic opaque message such as 'Service temporarily unavailable' or an error code

ce26dedccb07.summary: Same internal-detail leak in seller validation error message
ce26dedccb07.fix: Same â€” use an opaque error message

426d75bd6ea4.summary: Entire validation layer is unimplemented; all buyer and seller actions are unvalidated
426d75bd6ea4.exploit: Any production deployment of this stub allows price, quantity, and action values to flow through with zero enforcement â€” negative prices, zero quantities, or unauthorized actions are never rejected
426d75bd6ea4.fix: Block deployment until both functions are fully implemented and return real ValidationResult values

3134027b9e56.summary: humanConfirmed flag is never checked; human-in-the-loop invariant can be silently violated
3134027b9e56.exploit: Pass humanConfirmed=false for a high-value 'accept' or 'settle' action â€” stub throws before enforcing the flag, so any error-swallowing middleware allows the action to proceed without human approval
3134027b9e56.fix: Implement an explicit guard: if (!humanConfirmed && ['accept','settle'].includes(action)) return {valid:false, reason:'requires human confirmation'}

565188a0f791.summary: Function always throws, any caller will crash at runtime
565188a0f791.exploit: Call writeBuyerAudit() with any valid BuyerAuditEntry â€” unconditional throw propagates to caller
565188a0f791.fix: Implement the function body or use a no-op stub instead of throwing

c0b0bef25a60.summary: Function always throws, any caller will crash at runtime
c0b0bef25a60.exploit: Call writeSellerAudit() with any valid SellerAuditEntry â€” unconditional throw propagates to caller
c0b0bef25a60.fix: Implement the function body or use a no-op stub instead of throwing

7d64d20b7e82.summary: Audit trail is entirely non-functional â€” security events are never recorded
7d64d20b7e82.exploit: Trigger any auditable action (bid, negotiation, settlement); no audit log is written, violations go undetected
7d64d20b7e82.fix: Implement or wire a real audit sink before deploying; failing open on audit is a compliance/forensics risk

ce37a6aa96f0.summary: Stub always throws; no validation ever executes, any input passes type-cast unsanitized if caller catches the error
ce37a6aa96f0.exploit: Caller wraps in try/catch and falls back to raw cast: the thrown error is swallowed and untrusted data flows downstream as OpenRequest
ce37a6aa96f0.fix: Implement full Zod/ajv schema validation before the function is deployed; block deployment of stub in production via CI gate

5d60bb2827cb.summary: Same stub failure â€” CounterRequest validation never runs
5d60bb2827cb.exploit: Same catch-and-cast pattern against raw untrusted input
5d60bb2827cb.fix: Implement validation; add pre-deployment CI check that stubs are replaced

724980e82e99.summary: Same stub failure â€” AcceptRequest validation never runs
724980e82e99.exploit: Same catch-and-cast pattern against raw untrusted input
724980e82e99.fix: Implement validation; add pre-deployment CI check that stubs are replaced

671def533296.summary: Same stub failure â€” WalkRequest validation never runs
671def533296.exploit: Same catch-and-cast pattern against raw untrusted input
671def533296.fix: Implement validation; add pre-deployment CI check that stubs are replaced

b2b7018f2bb5.summary: Same stub failure â€” StatusRequest validation never runs
b2b7018f2bb5.exploit: Same catch-and-cast pattern against raw untrusted input
b2b7018f2bb5.fix: Implement validation; add pre-deployment CI check that stubs are replaced

634b11ec2a79.summary: All parse functions unconditionally throw; any caller without explicit error handling will crash the process
634b11ec2a79.exploit: Call any parse function without try/catch in an async Express route handler â€” unhandled promise rejection crashes or hangs the server
634b11ec2a79.fix: Implement functions; document throwing contract; ensure all call sites wrap in try/catch or use a centralized error boundary

f53f364fda12.summary: Stub functions make the system appear functional at compile time but are permanently broken at runtime, violating the contract between Developer 1 and consuming code
f53f364fda12.exploit: Deploy to staging/prod before Developer 1 delivers; every negotiation endpoint becomes a 500 with no useful error to the client
f53f364fda12.fix: Add a TODO-blocking lint rule or integration test that fails CI if any parse function still throws 'not implemented'

dc53a2d7eafb.summary: Error message exposes internal developer ownership model ('Developer 1') and file structure to any caller who surfaces the raw exception message
dc53a2d7eafb.exploit: Trigger any parse call, catch the Error, and return error.message directly in an API response â€” leaks team/file internals
dc53a2d7eafb.fix: Use a generic 'not implemented' message without internal attribution; strip raw error messages from API responses

96009440883c.summary: expires_at is optional; omitting it produces a signed envelope valid forever, enabling indefinite replay
96009440883c.exploit: Send a NuffEnvelope without expires_at; the signature remains valid and the message can be re-submitted at any future time
96009440883c.fix: Make expires_at required and enforce a maximum validity window at parse time

6d4884b81cc7.summary: signature is an untyped string with no algorithm binding; empty string or algorithm-confusion attacks are type-valid
6d4884b81cc7.exploit: Submit signature:"" or switch signing algorithm (e.g. HMAC-SHA256 instead of Ed25519); no field rejects either
6d4884b81cc7.fix: Add a required sig_alg field with a locked literal type (e.g. "ed25519") and reject empty signatures at validation boundary

5c6459faaf72.summary: payment_required.pay_to is an unvalidated string; seller can substitute an arbitrary or attacker-controlled address
5c6459faaf72.exploit: Seller returns pay_to:"0xattacker" or pay_to:""; buyer's agent pays the wrong address with no type-level rejection
5c6459faaf72.fix: Constrain pay_to with a branded type or regex pattern enforced at the validation boundary (e.g. EIP-55 checksum address)

22ef6ded9f1e.summary: cap field in audit log exposes buyer's maximum price; if logs reach the seller, BATNA is fully revealed
22ef6ded9f1e.exploit: Seller reads audit log entries (e.g. via shared storage, log aggregator, or compromised log pipeline) and learns cap, then demands exactly that price in every counter
22ef6ded9f1e.fix: Remove cap from the exported audit type or replace it with a boolean hit_cap; store the raw value only in a buyer-private log

e385920809f9.summary: floor field in audit log exposes seller's minimum price; buyer learning it can offer exactly floor and extract maximum surplus
e385920809f9.exploit: Buyer reads SellerAuditEntry (e.g. via shared log storage) and learns floor, then makes a take-it-or-leave-it offer at floor+1
e385920809f9.fix: Remove floor from the exported audit type or replace with a boolean at_floor boolean flag

b6290722dca0.summary: settlement_url is an unconstrained string; seller can return internal network or metadata-service URLs
b6290722dca0.exploit: Seller sets settlement_url:"http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/" ; buyer agent fetches it, leaking cloud credentials
b6290722dca0.fix: Validate settlement_url at parse time against an allowlist of schemes/hosts; reject non-HTTPS or RFC-1918 targets

0525c246fb53.summary: max_price, target_price, and all number price fields accept NaN and Infinity; NaN poisons all comparisons silently
0525c246fb53.exploit: Send max_price:NaN; every guard of the form price <= max_price evaluates to false, bypassing the cap entirely
0525c246fb53.fix: Add a branded PositiveFiniteNumber type validated at ingress with Number.isFinite(v) && v > 0

e5b436725f8e.summary: round: number accepts negative values and non-integers, breaking the round-based state machine
e5b436725f8e.exploit: Send round:-1 or round:0.5; state machine comparisons behave unexpectedly, potentially allowing out-of-order processing
e5b436725f8e.fix: Validate that round is a non-negative integer (Number.isInteger && v >= 0) at envelope parse time

59c4101b651f.summary: No type constraint that floor_price <= preferred_price <= opening_ask; inverted values produce irrational seller behaviour
59c4101b651f.exploit: Set floor_price:1000, opening_ask:1; seller accepts any offer above 1 but will never walk even below floor
59c4101b651f.fix: Add a runtime invariant check (or Zod refinement) asserting floor_price <= preferred_price <= opening_ask

9023246fac39.summary: accepted_price is unconstrained; buyer can accept at a price never offered, including 0 or negative
9023246fac39.exploit: Send AcceptRequest with accepted_price:0 after a negotiation; type system does not prevent accepting at an off-protocol price
9023246fac39.fix: Validate accepted_price matches the last counter price on record before processing the accept

e8999ffa062b.summary: require_human_confirmation_above accepts 0 or negative, making every transaction trigger human confirmation, or can be set to Infinity to bypass all confirmations
e8999ffa062b.exploit: Set require_human_confirmation_above:-1 to gate every single micro-payment on human approval, or Infinity to silently skip confirmation on any amount
e8999ffa062b.fix: Validate that require_human_confirmation_above is a positive finite number when present

9daac9481546.summary: allow_partial_match:true is expressible but OpenResponse and AcceptResponse have no quantity_filled field; partial fills are silently undetectable
9daac9481546.exploit: Seller accepts a partial quantity (e.g. 3 of 10 units) but the response type carries no quantity field; buyer agent pays full amount for partial delivery
9daac9481546.fix: Add quantity_filled: number to OpenResponse and AcceptResponse, required when allow_partial_match is true

46833b646d96.summary: note is a free-form string with no length or character restrictions; can inject CRLF or structured-log tokens
46833b646d96.exploit: Send note:"\n2026-01-01 INFO deal accepted price=0" to forge a fake audit log entry in newline-delimited log systems
46833b646d96.fix: Sanitize or reject note values containing CR/LF/NUL at validation boundary; enforce a max length (e.g. 256 chars)

b92ad5df9e93.summary: intent_summary is an unbounded string that may be written verbatim to logs, enabling log forging
b92ad5df9e93.exploit: Send intent_summary containing CRLF sequences or JSON-breaking characters to inject false log lines
b92ad5df9e93.fix: Sanitize intent_summary at ingress; strip control characters and enforce a maximum length

2066e42719ec.summary: DealPhase is a plain union with no transition table; type system permits arbitrary phase jumps (open -> settled)
2066e42719ec.exploit: Construct a StatusResponse with phase:"settled" for a deal still in phase:"open"; no type guard prevents illegal transitions
2066e42719ec.fix: Enforce a state transition map at runtime (e.g. allowedTransitions[current].includes(next)) before mutating phase

56f51c7367a2.summary: max_rounds: number accepts Infinity or MAX_SAFE_INTEGER, enabling unbounded negotiation loops
56f51c7367a2.exploit: Set max_rounds:Infinity; negotiation agent loops forever without terminating, exhausting compute or API budget
56f51c7367a2.fix: Validate max_rounds is a positive integer within a reasonable ceiling (e.g. <= 100) at ingress

e0903bf44239.summary: accepted:false with reason_code optional means rejections can carry no actionable information
e0903bf44239.exploit: Seller returns {accepted:false} with no reason_code or counter_price; buyer agent has no basis to counter and may silently walk
e0903bf44239.fix: Make reason_code required when accepted is false, using a discriminated union: {accepted:false; reason_code: string}

df4413d14672.summary: timestamp and expires_at are untyped strings with no ISO-8601 format enforcement; malformed or past dates are type-valid
df4413d14672.exploit: Send timestamp:"not-a-date"; downstream Date.parse returns NaN, making expiry and ordering checks silently incorrect
df4413d14672.fix: Use a branded ISODateString type validated with a strict regex or Date.parse check at ingress

96d105b93a4f.summary: SELLER_PUBKEY is a hardcoded literal 'mock-seller-pubkey' providing no real cryptographic seller identity verification
96d105b93a4f.exploit: Any server at SELLER_URL trivially passes pubkey checks because the expected key is always the well-known string 'mock-seller-pubkey'; a MitM or rogue seller is indistinguishable from the legitimate one
96d105b93a4f.fix: Load seller pubkey from a secure env var or config file and verify TLS/message signatures against the real key

c0ba1b58e3f0.summary: SELLER_URL env var is passed to runBuyerNegotiation without scheme or host validation, enabling SSRF to internal services
c0ba1b58e3f0.exploit: SELLER_URL=http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/ in a cloud environment causes the buyer agent to exfiltrate instance metadata credentials
c0ba1b58e3f0.fix: Validate SELLER_URL against an allowlist of permitted schemes and hostnames before passing it to the negotiation function

48115d73de95.summary: runBuyerNegotiation is called immediately after app.listen() without awaiting the 'listening' event, so the first request can arrive before the socket is bound
48115d73de95.exploit: On a loaded system the buyer agent's first HTTP request races the TCP bind and receives ECONNREFUSED, causing the negotiation to fail non-deterministically
48115d73de95.fix: Wrap app.listen() in a promise that resolves on the 'listening' event and await it before calling runBuyerNegotiation

b1c68694adf7.summary: PORT env var is cast with Number() but never range-validated; NaN, 0, negative, or >65535 values silently reach app.listen()
b1c68694adf7.exploit: PORT=abc yields SELLER_PORT=NaN and sellerUrl='http://localhost:NaN'; PORT=99999 is an invalid port; both produce silent misconfiguration or an unhandled listen error
b1c68694adf7.fix: After parsing, assert Number.isInteger(SELLER_PORT) && SELLER_PORT >= 1 && SELLER_PORT <= 65535 and throw a descriptive error otherwise

6063e253d618.summary: opening_offer is hardcoded at 4 while target_price is Math.min(4,maxPrice); when maxPrice<4 the buyer opens above their own price ceiling
6063e253d618.exploit: BUYER_MAX_PRICE=3 â†’ target_price=3 but strategy.opening_offer=4; the buyer's first bid exceeds their stated maximum, violating their own mandate
6063e253d618.fix: Derive opening_offer dynamically as Math.min(4, maxPrice) so it never exceeds the buyer's ceiling

040cab2f40b6.summary: result.txHash and result.artifact from the external seller server are interpolated directly into console.log without sanitization
040cab2f40b6.exploit: Seller returns txHash containing '\n[Settled] FAKE_ENTRY\n[Admin] payment-redirected' to inject spurious log lines that deceive operators or downstream log parsers
040cab2f40b6.fix: Strip or encode newlines and control characters from externally-sourced strings before interpolating them into log messages

e6bc1886ee61.summary: Local seller server is unconditionally started even when SELLER_URL env var overrides the endpoint, wasting a port binding and running a live HTTP service unnecessarily
e6bc1886ee61.exploit: Set SELLER_URL to an external endpoint; the local server still binds SELLER_PORT, accepts connections, and processes requests even though the buyer never talks to it
e6bc1886ee61.fix: Conditionally start the local server only when SELLER_URL is not set in the environment

2d8b254022b1.summary: If server.close() rejects inside the finally block, it throws and replaces any in-flight negotiation error, making the root cause invisible
2d8b254022b1.exploit: A negotiation failure followed by a server close error causes the outer catch to see only the server close error; the original business failure is lost and process.exitCode may be set for the wrong reason
2d8b254022b1.fix: Catch the server.close() rejection independently (e.g., with .catch(console.error)) so it never masks the primary exception

2e00f135e9cc.summary: When a non-Error rejection reaches the top-level catch, the raw value is passed to console.error, potentially printing internal objects with sensitive fields
2e00f135e9cc.exploit: A rejected promise carrying {apiKey:'sk-live-...', status:'failed'} prints the full object to stderr where it may be captured by log aggregators
2e00f135e9cc.fix: Always serialize unknown thrown values safely: String(error) or JSON.stringify with a replacer that redacts known sensitive keys
